name: cpu_video_encode
title: Software video encoding
short_description: H.264 software encode of a bundled 1080p clip using x264.

structured_text.description: Encodes a bundled 1080p video clip to H.264 entirely on the processor using the x264 encoder at a fixed quality preset. Software encoding is chosen on purpose so the test measures the CPU rather than a fixed function hardware encoder. The clip and settings are identical on every machine.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the average number of frames encoded per second across the clip, reported in fps.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More frames per second means faster exports and smoother previews when an effects chain forces software encoding.
structured_text.reasoning: High quality software encoding is one of the most demanding sustained jobs a creator runs, which makes it the right headline test for video work.

domain_gaming.explanation: Streamers encode gameplay, often falling back to the CPU when the GPU is busy.

domain_office.explanation: Office users encode video only occasionally for presentations.

domain_software_development.explanation: Developers rarely run sustained video encoding.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Motion graphics work produces video that needs encoding at export.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Software encoding speed directly sets how long a final export takes.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Animation output is encoded to deliverable video after frames are rendered.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work touches video encoding only when scoring to picture.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Engineering work occasionally encodes simulation flythroughs.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Encoding is a minor step when turning simulation output into video.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Video datasets are sometimes transcoded during preparation.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Pipelines that produce video output need an encode stage.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Media servers run software transcoding when hardware encoders are unavailable.
